title: Kokarneswarar Temple, Thirukokarnam
tagline: Kokarneswarar Temple, Thirukokarnam — spiritual town of Tamil Nadu

overview.short: The Kokarneswarar Temple is situated in town of Thirukokarnam located 5 kilometres from the town of Pudukkottai in the Tamil Nadu, India.
overview.description: The Kokarneswarar Temple is situated in town of Thirukokarnam located 5 kilometres from the town of Pudukkottai in the Tamil Nadu, India. The presiding deity is Kokarneswarar considered to be a form of the Hindu God Shiva. His consort Brihadambal is the family deity of the Thondaiman kings which ruled the princely Pudukkottai state. The Pallava-era rock-cut temple was constructed by the Pallava king Mahendravarman I sometime around the middle of the 7th century CE. Later additions were made in the early 2nd millennium by the Chola and Pandya dynasties.

topPlaces.name: Government Museum, Pudukkottai
topPlaces.description: This Government Museum is a museum located in the town of Pudukkottai of Pudukkottai District. This is the second largest museum of Tamil Nadu after Government Museum, Chennai.

topPlaces.name: Pudukkottai
topPlaces.description: Pudukkottai is a City and the administrative headquarters of Pudukkottai district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. It is one of the oldest heritage cities located on the banks of the Vellar River.

topPlaces.name: Nathampannai
topPlaces.description: Nathampannai is a panchayat town in Pudukkottai district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu.

topPlaces.name: Pudukkottai taluk
topPlaces.description: Pudukkottai taluk is a taluk of Pudukkottai district of the Indian state of Tamil Nadu.

topPlaces.name: Thiruvengaivasal
topPlaces.description: Thiruvengaivasal is a village in the Annavasal revenue block of Pudukkottai district, Tamil Nadu, India.

topPlaces.name: Vyagrapurisvarar Temple, Thiruvengaivasal
topPlaces.description: Vyagrapurisvarar Temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to the deity Shiva, located at Thiruvengaivasal of Pudukkottai district in Tamil Nadu, India.

topPlaces.name: Perumanadu
topPlaces.description: Perumanadu is a village in the Annavasalrevenue block of Pudukkottai district, Tamil Nadu, India.

topPlaces.name: Panchavarnaswamy Temple
topPlaces.description: Panchavarnaswamy Temple (An ancient Tamil temple) (பஞ்சவர்ணசுவாமி கோயில்) (usually Panjavarnaswamy Temple) is a Tamil temple dedicated to Shiva, located in Woraiyur, a neighborhood in the town of Tiruchirapalli in Tamil Nadu, India. Shiva is believed to portray five different colours, giving the name of the presiding deity, Panchavarnaswamy.

Panchavarnaswamy Temple (An ancient Tamil temple) (பஞ்சவர்ணசுவாமி கோயில்) (usually Panjavarnaswamy Temple) is a Tamil temple dedicated to Shiva, located in Woraiyur, a neighborhood in the town of Tiruchirapalli in Tamil Nadu, India. Shiva is believed to portray five different colours, giving the name of the presiding deity, Panchavarnaswamy.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Kokarneswarar Temple, Thirukokarnam?
faq.a: The best time to visit Kokarneswarar Temple, Thirukokarnam is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Kokarneswarar Temple, Thirukokarnam?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Tiruchirappalli International Airport (43 km) and the nearest railway station is Tiruchirappalli Junction (47 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Kokarneswarar Temple, Thirukokarnam?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Kokarneswarar Temple, Thirukokarnam at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Kokarneswarar Temple, Thirukokarnam?
faq.a: Stays in Kokarneswarar Temple, Thirukokarnam start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Kokarneswarar Temple, Thirukokarnam?
faq.a: The top attractions are Government Museum, Pudukkottai, Pudukkottai, Nathampannai, Pudukkottai taluk, Thiruvengaivasal.
